decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the game through efficient removal and disruptive spells, while utilizing strong creatures like Krosan Tusker and Ravenous Baloth to apply pressure when needed. The use of cards like Pernicious Deed and Cabal Therapy help to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent?s strategy. The inclusion of cards like Dust Bowl and Treetop Village provide additional utility and versatility in dealing with various threats. This deck aims to outvalue the opponent by outmaneuvering and outlasting them in a battle of attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to efficiently combo off by using Tinker to tutor for Phyrexian Devourer, then sacrificing it to Altar of Dementia to mill your opponent's entire library. This combo is supported by cards like Lotus Petal and Mox Diamond to accelerate your mana production, as well as Oath of Druids to potentially dig for key pieces. Defense Grid is also included to protect your combo from disruption, while Fling serves as the finishing move to deal lethal damage. Overall, the goal is to assemble the combo quickly and overwhelm your opponent before they can respond effectively.

The focus of this deck is to assemble the combo of Tinker and Fling to quickly and efficiently win the game. It utilizes cards like Phyrexian Devourer, Altar of Dementia, and Defense Grid to set up the combo while disrupting opponents with cards like Tangle Wire and Tsabo's Web. The deck also includes mana acceleration with cards like Mind Stone and Ancient Tomb to quickly assemble the combo pieces. Overall, the deck aims to quickly set up and execute the Tinker Fling combo to take down opponents in a Premodern 1 vs 1 matchup.

This deck focuses on quickly playing aggressive creatures like Basking Rootwalla and Wild Mongrel, while utilizing Madness cards like Circular Logic and Careful Study to keep up card advantage. The deck also includes cards like Aquamoeba and Wonder to provide evasion and additional threats. The deck is designed to pressure the opponent with early aggression and card advantage through Madness mechanics, while using counterspells like Daze and Circular Logic to protect its threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using cycling cards to generate card advantage and board presence while managing the opponent's threats. Cards like Eternal Dragon and Renewed Faith provide constant value, while spells like Swords to Plowshares and Decree of Justice can deal with any threats that come up. The deck aims to outlast opponents with its efficient removal and cycling engine, ultimately winning the game through card advantage and resource management.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a swarm of aggressive Goblin creatures. By using cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to bring out powerful Goblin creatures like Goblin Ringleader and Goblin Warchief, the deck can quickly build a strong board presence. Gempalm Incinerator also provides a way to deal with opposing creatures while drawing more Goblins. Utilizing cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port, the deck can disrupt the opponent's mana base and maintain control of the game. The key to victory lies in fast, relentless aggression.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a lock on the game by utilizing the card Stasis along with other control and draw spells. By combining these cards together, the deck aims to restrict the opponent's ability to untap their lands, effectively stalling their game plan while allowing the player to maintain control. The inclusion of bounce spells like Chain of Vapor and counterspells help to protect the Stasis lock, while also providing disruption to the opponent's strategy. The deck prioritizes card selection and efficiency in order to ensure a consistent and powerful lock on the game.

This deck focuses on disrupting your opponent's strategy while building up your own board presence through growing creatures like Quirion Dryad and Mystic Enforcer. By using efficient counterspells and removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to control the game and outvalue the opponent. Additionally, the deck includes card draw and mana ramp to ensure that you can consistently play threats and answers. With a mix of disruption, card advantage, and powerful creatures, this deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through countering spells, removal, and board wipes until it can establish a strong advantage. It uses Standstill to slow down the opponent's game plan while taking advantage of its own card advantage engines like Fact or Fiction and Impulse. The win condition usually comes from grinding the opponent out by controlling the board state with land destruction and incremental advantage until it can deploy threats like Exalted Angel or win with Decree of Justice. The deck aims to outlast and outvalue the opponent in a grindy game.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By playing efficient creatures like Grim Lavamancer and Mogg Fanatic alongside powerful spells like Incinerate and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to deal damage swiftly and efficiently. Quirion Dryad grows in power as more spells are played, adding to the deck's aggressive strategy. The inclusion of cards like Call of the Herd and Sylvan Library provide additional threats and card advantage to keep the pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes utility cards like Naturalize and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive low-cost creatures and direct burn spells. It aims to maintain a fast-paced offense by consistently dealing damage to the opponent's life total while controlling the board with efficient removal spells. The presence of cards like Grim Lavamancer and Sulfuric Vortex ensures a continuous source of damage, while the inclusion of cards like Seal of Fire and Cursed Scroll provide added flexibility in dealing with threats. Overall, the deck is designed to apply constant pressure and end the game quickly.

The deck revolves around utilizing Stasis as a lock piece to deny the opponent resources. By combining stalling tactics such as Portent and Forsaken City with countermagic and bounce spells, the deck aims to control the game until it can assemble a winning combo. This combo could involve milling the opponent with Brain Freeze or chaining spells with Gush and Foil. With a focus on disrupting the opponent's game plan and establishing a hard lock, the deck seeks to outmaneuver and overwhelm the opponent in a battle of wits and resource management.

The focus of this deck is to utilize the powerful combo between Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to quickly put a formidable threat on the battlefield. The deck also includes cards to control the game with counterspells and draw spells, ensuring the combo can be efficiently assembled while disrupting the opponent's strategy. Additionally, there are a few cards like Chain of Vapor and Brain Freeze for alternate win conditions or additional disruption. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its efficient combo and control elements in a Premodern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck is focused on controlling the game through a combination of creature removal, hand disruption, and board wipes. It utilizes a mix of efficient creatures like Ravenous Baloth and Wall of Roots to maintain board presence, while cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress disrupt the opponent's hand. Pernicious Deed and Recurring Nightmare provide additional ways to deal with threats on the board. The mana base allows for consistent access to both green and black sources, while also providing utility with lands like Treetop Village and Dust Bowl. Overall, the deck aims to grind out the opponent and outvalue them in the long game.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively play creatures with the Madness ability, allowing for efficient and unexpected plays. By discarding cards for their Madness cost and utilizing cards like Circular Logic to control the game, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and versatile playstyle. The inclusion of powerful creatures like Arrogant Wurm and Wild Mongrel, along with card draw engines like Frantic Search and Careful Study, ensures that the deck can maintain pressure and card advantage throughout the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost, aggressive creatures like Basking Rootwalla and Wild Mongrel to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The Madness mechanic is heavily utilized with cards like Circular Logic and Careful Study to gain card advantage and tempo. The inclusion of powerful Madness cards like Arrogant Wurm and Roar of the Wurm allow for explosive plays and a strong finish. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan with counter spells like Daze and controlling card advantage with Deep Analysis.
